experience.title: Founder/CEO
experience.description:
Building TravWiz to fix the broken trip planning experience.

After spending hours planning multiple vacations using generic AI chatbots (and still missing key attractions due to sold-out tickets) I realized these tools give information but don't provide intelligence. They tell you what to do, but don't guide you step-by-step from start to finish.

TravWiz bridges this gap with AI-powered trip planning that actually works:
- Smart booking alerts (what to book now vs. day-of)
- Seasonal recommendations (right time, not just right place)  
- Realistic itineraries with breaks built in for food, drinks, etc. (not 15-activity marathons)
- Budget transparency (know exactly what you'll spend)

Bootstrapped from the ground up. Using 25 years of product and engineering experience to build what I wish existed when planning my own vacations.

Currently in beta with 40+ travelers, planning vacations in 100+ destinations worldwide. Building features based on real user feedback, shipping fast, and learning in public.

If you're passionate about travel tech, product development, or AI-assisted planning, I'd love to connect.

DM me for Beta access

headline: Engineering Leadership  at Facebook | Technologist | Innovator | Org Builder | Diversity Champion | Talent Coach | Speaker
about:
A technology leader with over 15 years of experience, encompassing both product-driven R&D, rooted in ML/AI, and go-to-market. Proven track record of collaborative leadership skills, cross-functional influencing, providing technical vision & strategy, balancing short- and long-term technology investments. Solid execution experience at large scale - 1B+ users, multi-billion dollar revenue - and under uncertainty and ambiguity (including several 0 -> 1 initiatives) having worked in high-energy, fast-paced environments.

Experience with building & scaling teams - with strong focus on diversity, equity and inclusion - ground up and growing next-gen leaders through empowerment and coaching.

experience.title: Engineering Leader
experience.description:
Currently, in Reality Labs, leading a team to define and ship novel user experiences across Meta's device portfolio - both MR and Wearables. 

Previously, in Ads, led two large programs, spanning over a dozen teams across multiple orgs, geared towards revenue reliability - enable early assessment of production-riskiness of a change <code, config, model, data> and early detection of production issues to mitigate impact on revenue and advertiser experience. The programs landed a reduction in revenue loss by over a billion dollars.

Previously, led Business/Content Integrity efforts - set technical vision and coached engineers and managers to accelerate their growth and learning. Build robust integrity support, rooted in state-of-the-art ML/(Generative) AI models across different modalities, for a variety of domains such as anti-counterfeit (fraud), political ads and dynamic ads. Shipped multiple 0 -> 1 products - e.g., integrity support to ensure compliance with UK FCA regulations - in deep collaboration with partners in Product Management, Product Marketing, Data Science, Operations, Policy, Sales and Legal.
                                                 
Previously, led a team  (scaled 4x, including senior external hires) of engineers and research scientists to drive efficiency across the key product groups -- Ads, Feed, Shops -- at Meta. Responsible for annual capex planning -- of over a billion dollars -- for the aforementioned product groups. The team developed a large suite of novel algorithmic optimizations to land material wins across the entire spectrum, i.e., from optimizing deep learning models to low-level optimizations, performance modeling of deep learning models. The team has delivered CapEx savings of over a billion dollars over four years and advanced state-of-the-art. Some of the work has been published in top-tier peer-reviewed forums such as, but not limited to, ICLR, NeurIPS, MLSys, KDD, UAI, ICMLA, ISCA, ICPE.

experience.title: R&D Leader
experience.description:
Co-founded MZ Research (MZR), with the CTO, and built a cross-organizational team -- across the verticals Cognant and Satori -- of exceptional researchers from the ground up. Set the vision, strategy and roadmap for the  various product-driven research initiatives. On a lifetime basis, MZR landed business impact of over $100M (!), filed over a dozen patents and drove material optimization of marketing  campaigns of customers of Cognant -- a large set of leading multi-billion dollar brands -- thereby boosting their bottomline. MZR led research initiatives on multiple fronts, viz.,

* R&D of novel machine learning and data mining techniques for detecting fraud - impression, click, install & engagement - in marketing data. The team has developed state-of-the-art fraud detection technology which has had material impact on the overall marketing efficiency.

* R&D of techniques for optimizing mobile marketing campaigns, e.g., but not limited to, algorithmic bidding, assessing the efficacy of TV Ad campaigns, publisher quality analysis, LTV prediction, budget allocation, creative and audience recommendation.

* R&D of techniques for analysis of live data streams. For instance, but not limited to, detecting anomalies and segmenting, classifying and summarization (such as count by class type) of live data streams.

* R&D of techniques for detection of potential security threats, bot detection and near real-time anomaly detection in operational data.

experience.title: Research Engineer
experience.description:
Led R&D of novel techniques for anomaly detection in client events and other metrics consumed by data partners of Twitter. The corresponding tool, called Oddduck, is being used by more than 10 teams at Twitter. Developed techniques to detect ``outlier"​ nodes in large (>5K) production clusters -- the techniques are being used by SREs of multiple teams such Hadoop, data stores such as Manhattan.

Interfaced with cross-functional teams to evaluate the capacity needs based on organic growth and product roadmap. Partnered with teams to improve operational efficiency. Established the vision and provided technical leadership to build the flagship product, called Chiffchaff, used by a large number of services at Twitter for statistical analysis of system and application metrics and forecasting of business metrics. Technical lead for capacity planning for events such as New Year, Superbowl, FIFA World Cup 2014.

Hired and mentored interns to develop novel algorithms for anomaly detection and shepherded new hires to develop algorithms for breakout detection based on statistical learning. The algorithms developed have been deployed in production. The corresponding R packages have been open sourced on GitHub. 

Invited to leading industry conferences such as QCon (NYC'13) and Velocity (Santa Clara'13, '14, '15, NYC'14, '15, Barcelona'14, London'13) and academic conferences for giving talks. Published research papers in international peer-reviewed conferences.

experience.title: Senior Cloud Performance Engineer
experience.description:
Developed a state-of-the-art Java GC Log Analysis Engine to guide the tuning of GC performance on clusters of thousands of nodes. The metrics reported by the engine were mined based on the statistical analysis of the time series of various GC performance parameters. The engine supported both CMS and G1 garbage collectors. A tools paper was published in a peer-reviewed international conference. 

Developed methodologies for load testing (using JMeter) and capacity planning for Netflix applications. Also, developed various autoscaling models to reduce operational costs and improve efficiency on the cloud. The models were deployed in production and were instrumental in reducing the capacity footprint of several applications by over 50%. A research paper was published in a peer-reviewed international conference. Developed techniques to learn insights about user behavior by statistical log mining (stored in Hive).

Co-lead the on-boarding of various application teams for adoption of autoscaling and scalability analysis using JMeter.

experience.title: Senior Software Systems Engineer
experience.description:
Worked on gamut of projects in Yahoo! Search Engine, Global Search Middleware, In-Node Tiering. Broadly, the projects entailed the following: (a) performance, scalability analysis and optimization of Yahoo! properties on large clusters comprising of multi-core nodes; (b) workload characterization; (c) performance modeling using hardware performance counters. (d) compiler-driven and algorithmic program optimization/parallelization.

Co-developed a state-of-the-art dynamic call graph extraction framework to facilitate program analysis and optimization. Mentored an intern for research and development of novel techniques geared towards assessing program similarity. Filed several patent applications and published research papers in international peer-reviewed conferences and workshops.

school.name: Classes Préparatoires du Lycée Janson de Sailly

education.description: The classes préparatoires aux grandes écoles (CPGE), commonly called classes prépas or prépas, are part of the French post-secondary education system. They consist in two very selective years (extensible to three-year), acting as a prep course (or cram school) with the main goal of training undergraduate students for enrolment in one of the grandes écoles. The workload is generally very high and in-class competition between students is often encouraged. Also, in these classes, grades are usually very low. Teachers do this to intimidate students and keep only the best of them in the second year.

education.activities: VP of students council
education.description: In France, the majority of managers and executives in both industry and commerce are graduates of the "Grandes Écoles". More than 60% of the presidents and managing directors of the 100 largest French companies are mostly graduates from these academic institutions.

headline: GM‑Level Builder & Operator | Engineering + Product + Commercial + Operations | Stabilisation, Scale‑Up & Capability Uplift | Open to Executive & Interim Roles
about:
I’m a GM‑level builder and operator who creates alignment across engineering, product, commercial and operational functions. My best work happens in organisations that need clarity, capability uplift and a scalable operating model - whether they’re growing, transforming or stabilising.

Across mining technology, healthcare tech, transport and industrial systems, I’ve led multi‑site and global teams through periods of complexity and change. I’m typically brought in when an organisation needs someone who can connect strategy to delivery, rebuild confidence, and shape the systems that enable sustainable performance.

What I’m known for:
- Creating alignment across engineering, product, commercial and operations
- Building capability and leadership depth in technical and operational teams
- Designing operating models that scale
- Restoring clarity in complex or ambiguous environments
- Leading cross‑functional teams through growth, transformation or stabilisation
- Balancing commercial, customer and operational realities

I’ve led $20M–$100M business units and cross‑functional teams across multiple sectors, always with a focus on building organisations that can perform without heroics or dependency.

Open to GM, Director and Head Of roles (permanent or interim), and advisory work where alignment, clarity and forward momentum are needed.

experience.title: Head of Product - Integrations & Interoperability
experience.description:
Brought in to stabilise critical national digital health integrations, restore customer confidence, and rebuild delivery discipline across a complex, multi‑product environment.

- Led integration and interoperability strategy across a global healthcare product suite, improving compliance with national digital health requirements and strengthening platform reliability.
- Stabilised underperforming accounts by introducing clearer governance, commercial structures and executive‑level engagement, reducing escalations and improving client trust.
- Lifted capability and leadership depth within product teams, improving planning, customer engagement and cross‑functional alignment.
- Rebuilt delivery discipline across engineering, product and operations, enabling teams to prioritise effectively, sequence work, and deliver with greater predictability.

experience.title: Program Director (GM-level scope)
experience.description:
Brought in to commercialise a global AI platform, strengthen operational discipline, and scale a $20M business unit across multiple regions.

- Converted multi‑year R&D into commercial revenue, launching a global AI platform used across Tier‑1 mining markets and establishing a repeatable go‑to‑market model.
- Strengthened leadership capability, improving cross‑functional alignment, operational clarity and execution discipline across engineering, product, field services and commercial teams.
- Directed $20M P&L discipline, aligning nine senior leaders across engineering, product, operations and commercial; rebuilt forecasting accuracy and restored executive confidence in delivery.
- Scaled international operations, expanding into Chile and shaping the North America growth roadmap while simplifying offerings to focus on high‑margin, cloud and analytics products.

experience.title: Program Director (GM-level scope)
experience.description:
Led a $110M portfolio across Tier‑1 mining and rail, stabilising major accounts, rebuilding capability, and leading multi‑site operations across Australia and global teams.

- Stabilised critical accounts for South32, BHP, Glencore Coal, and India Rail (among others), restoring delivery confidence and rebuilding commercial and operational discipline.
- Led multi‑site operations across Brisbane, Newcastle, Sydney, Adelaide and Perth; seconded into engineering leadership to resolve systemic issues and strengthen technical capability.
- Fronted key accounts for Predix, CAS, Track-IQ portfolios; resolving escalations, protecting revenue, and rebuilding long-term customer trust.
- Directed a $110M portfolio of 30+ projects & programs, reporting into US leadership and executive steering committees; improved forecasting, financial governance and risk visibility.
- Scaled engineering capacity by coordinating 50+ offshore engineers with onshore teams, and established a PMO to support global delivery.
- Strengthened client partnerships through account planning, senior stakeholder engagement and operational recovery during pandemic‑affected operations.

experience.title: Global Product Program Manager (Operating Model Rebuild & Stabilisation)
experience.description:
Brought in to stabilise MedChart after years of stalled offshore development, poor quality and fragmented teams. Rebuilt the operating model, unified global delivery and later expanded into broader product initiatives across Asia‑Pacific.

- Stabilised a failing global product line, resolving quality issues, reconnecting offshore teams and restoring confidence across clinical, product and executive stakeholders.
- Rebuilt structure and cadence, introducing governance, planning, forecasting and cross‑functional alignment across engineering, product, installation and support.
- Led a large‑scale global delivery organisation, running the work of 11 squads and 110+ staff across Australia, New Zealand, India, the USA and the UK.
- Reorganised teams and operating model, integrating Canberra resources, splitting dysfunctional groups and embedding a unified delivery rhythm.

headline: Director of AI Engineering | People-First Leadership, Product-Driven AI
about:
I build and scale AI products that deliver real business impact, from prototype to production, combining strong engineering leadership with a deep understanding of business and product.
My work sits at the intersection of AI, GenAI, LLMs, agile leadership, and data-driven decision making.

I am currently Director of AI Engineering at Edelman, where I lead the teams responsible for our proprietary AI products. My role combines technical and product leadership: defining strategy, scaling engineering teams, building strong team cultures, and ensuring our AI solutions are robust, explainable, and production-ready. I work closely with product and stakeholders to translate business needs into AI solutions that operate at global scale.

My career started hands-on as a data scientist and evolved naturally into leading teams and complex initiatives. Before Edelman, I led digitalization and AI teams at Airbus, defining operating models and strategies that reduced issue resolution time by 20% and improved operational efficiency. Earlier, at Airbus Defence & Space, Repsol, and Accenture, I worked at the business–AI interface, delivering advanced analytics and machine learning systems with measurable impact, from multimillion-euro margin improvements to large-scale production gains.

Across industries, my work has consistently focused on:
- Building AI products end-to-end
- Translating business needs into AI solutions
- Scaling engineering teams and strengthening team building practices
- Leading high-performing engineering teams through agile leadership
- Taking AI from experimentation to reliable, production-grade systems

My key learnings are simple: AI only creates value when teams are aligned, decisions are grounded in data, and solutions are realistic. I lead with pragmatism and determination, value clear communication, and prioritize delivery over hype.

Core strengths include AI and GenAI systems, LLM-based solutions, AI product development, scaling engineering teams, and bridging business, product, and engineering.

I am always open to sharing experiences and lessons learned, and I welcome opportunities to give technical talks or participate in AI-focused events.

headline: Technology Leadership Advisor and Executive Coach | Alignment, empathy, and decision-making in complex engineering organizations.
about:
I work with technology leaders and executive sponsors at the moments where responsibility expands faster than systems, expectations rise, and quiet risk begins to appear.

After more than 35 years inside technology organizations as an engineer, architect, and senior leader, I have seen a consistent pattern. The leaders who struggle most are often the strongest contributors. They are promoted because they are trusted and capable, then expected to think systemically, communicate clearly, and make high-stakes decisions without the structure or support to grow into those demands.

When that gap is ignored, the cost shows up as misalignment, stalled execution, burnout, and attrition. When it is addressed thoughtfully, organizations gain steadier leadership, better decisions, and stronger trust across teams.

My work focuses on leadership growth as a systems problem, not a personality exercise. I help organizations align leadership behavior, decision-making, and communication as technical responsibility increases.

I am the creator of the Unicorn’s Ascent Framework™, a structured approach that supports technical leaders as they move into broader organizational impact while giving executive sponsors clarity and confidence in how leadership is developing.

What I do:
• Executive and leadership coaching for technical leaders navigating increased scope and visibility
• Advisory work for executives supporting newly promoted or stretched technology leaders
• Speaking and facilitation for organizations exploring leadership, alignment, and decision-making under complexity

My style is calm, direct, and grounded in real operating experience. I create space for leaders to think clearly, name hard things without blame, and move forward with confidence and care for the people they lead.

If you are an executive sponsor sensing strain before it becomes visible, a technical leader navigating increased responsibility, or a meeting planner looking for substance over motivation, I’m always open to a thoughtful conversation.

With more than 35 years inside technology organizations as an engineer, architect, and senior leader, I bring deep technical context to my coaching and speaking work. I understand delivery pressure, organizational risk, and the human cost of misalignment because I have lived inside those systems.

My work is guided by the Unicorn’s Ascent Framework™, a structured approach that helps technical leaders grow into broader organizational responsibility without reducing leadership to personality traits or generic advice.

I support leaders and organizations in:
• Strengthening decision quality and leadership judgment

• Building trust and psychological safety under real pressure

Engagements include executive coaching, leadership workshops, and speaking programs for organizations ranging from early-stage teams to enterprise environments. The goal is consistent: help leaders and systems evolve together so people and performance can scale responsibly.

about: I lead engineering teams through the kind of change that defines companies. I enjoy building organizations that can ship fast, stay aligned, and grow with confidence. I often step into complex environments that need clarity and strong technical direction, and I build the teams and systems required to create impactful outcomes. This range of challenges shaped how I approach scale, execution, and long term performance.Today I serve as CTO at Collide, where we are building a fully AI-native enterprise platform for the industrial sector. We have built a domain-specific AI that outperforms leading foundation models on industry tasks, delivering intelligent document parsing, classification, and data extraction across complex data environments. More than any role before, this one has fundamentally changed how I think about building software. I have experienced firsthand what it means to lead an engineering organization through an AI transformation, not as a bolt-on, but as the foundation of how we build, ship, and operate. We rewired our entire engineering culture to be AI-first, adopting agentic coding tools like Claude Code and embedding AI-assisted workflows into daily development, while maintaining the architecture, security, and software standards that enterprise clients demand.I am known for the ability to create alignment in ambiguous environments and guide organizations through both technical and organizational transformation. I also provide fractional CTO advisory through RayJoy Technology Solutions, helping startups and scaleups define technical roadmaps, evaluate architectures, and build high-performing engineering teams.
